relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul meets the council with a claim of good conscience before the Divine.
The high priest answers by commanding a strike.
Paul exposes unlawful judgment hidden under lawful appearance.
The bystanders challenge his speech against the high priest.
Paul returns to Scripture about not speaking harmfully of rulers.
He names resurrection hope as the true issue.
Pharisee and Sadducee division opens.
The dispute includes resurrection, messenger, and spirit.
Some Pharisaic scribes find no harm and allow possible spirit-message.
Rome removes Paul from the tearing dispute.
The Lord stands by him: Jerusalem witness must become Rome witness.
A death-oath conspiracy forms at daybreak.
More than forty bind themselves to the plot.
The conspiracy seeks priestly cooperation.
A false request for examination hides ambush.
Paul’s nephew hears and enters the barracks.
Paul sends the young witness to the commander.
The centurion carries the report upward.
The commander privately receives the young man.
The plot is explained.
The ambush and oath are revealed before they can spring.
The commander protects the report’s secrecy.
A large night escort is prepared.
Paul is to be transferred safely to Felix.
A letter frames the case.
Claudius Lysias greets Felix.
The commander presents himself as rescuer of a Roman.
He sought to know the accusation.
The matter is judged as Law-questions, not death-worthy crime.
The plot requires immediate transfer and ordered accusation.
Night carries Paul to Antipatris.
Horsemen continue the escort.
Caesarea receives Paul and the letter.
Felix identifies Paul’s province.
Paul is held for a fuller hearing in Herod’s praetorium.
